segment-text: The Boy Next Door
EXT. EVANSTON, ILLINOIS - DAWN - AERIAL VIEW
A DEEP RAVINE slashes through a wooded area that stretches for miles behind picturesque homes. Through the treetops, we glimpse a FEMALE RUNNER on a trail.
EXT. WOODS - TRAIL - DAWN

MUDDY RUNNING SHOES pound a dirt path. We travel up toned legs and a trim frame to A FACE -- classically beautiful but set hard with determination. This is CLAIRE PETERSON, 39, though she could pass for years younger if she'd only lighten up a bit. She speeds up as she approaches a FALLEN SYCAMORE that blocks her path. With the grace of a gazelle she leaps over the downed tree and lands in ... A CLEARING poised on the brink of the ravine. Quiet. Mossy. A sylvan paradise, complete with a TIRE SWING hanging from a tree. Claire breathes heavily as she stretches against the fallen sycamore. Eventually, her gaze wanders to a WEATHERED CARVING on its trunk: "C.P. + G.P" encircled in a heart. Her eyes darken. Her watch BEEPS, pulling her back to the present. She hurries off toward a COLONIAL-STYLE HOME in the distance.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
Claire, dressed in a prim skirt and blouse, bustles about clearing the breakfast table. A NEWSCAST plays on a small TV, but Claire doesn't pay much attention to it.
In Milwaukee, a jury has convicted a local plumber for the murder of his live-in girlfriend, Marissa Sheldon.
(calling) Kevin! Time to go!

A photo of a PRETTY BLONDE WOMAN fills the TV screen. Claire gives it a fleeting glance while filling her commuter mug. NEWSCASTER A beloved teacher at Bay View High, thirty-one year-old Sheldon was found bludgeoned to death in her apartment last spring -Claire flicks off the TV.
(calling) Train's leaving, Kev!
Claire shoves a slice of burnt toast between her teeth, grabs her belongings, and goes out the door.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - ADJOINING GARAGE - MORNING
Claire enters from the house, juggling her things. She pushes the GARAGE DOOR OPENER on the wall, but the massive wooden door doesn't budge. She pushes it again. Nothing.
Oh, not today.
She sets her things down and lifts the door manually, struggling under its weight. But it immediately rolls back down, accompanied by a sprinkling of drywall. Damn it.
CLAIRE
KEVIN PETERSON, 13, gangly, Elmer's glue complexion, emerges from the house lugging a backpack and a CELLO CASE. He surveys the scene and reaches for his cell phone.
We better call Dad. No!
I've got it.
Claire wedges a ladder under the door.
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV - MORNING
With Kevin at her side, Claire checks her mirror and backs out. Instantly, they hear the sickening CRUNCH OF METAL.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - GARAGE - DAY
Claire gets out of the SUV and grimaces at the MANGLED LUGGAGE RACK on top, the garage door too low by a matter of millimeters. Kevin gets out, his eyes wide. Oh, man.
Just get in the car.
EXT. WILKERSON PREPARATORY SCHOOL - MORNING

A majestic brick building atop a sprawling lawn. SUVs stream through the gated entry decorated with a "Welcome Back!" banner. Claire's SUV pulls in, the luggage rack secured by a bungee cord and duct tape.
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV (MOVING) - DAY

Claire maneuvers through the parking lot where EXCITED TEENS greet each other after a long summer apart. Kevin spots ALLIE CAMBRIDGE, 17, beauty pageant material, laughing with girlfriends. Claire notices his eyes following her as they drive past. Claire starts to turn into a parking space when an acne-scarred tough-guy, JASON ZIMMER, 18, steps into her path, rough-housing with some boys. Kevin shrinks at the sight of Jason. pass, then parks. Claire waits for him to
You have any problem with him -Mom.
I can handle it.
Kevin gets out and trudges toward the building. Claire watches him disappear in her mirror, then gets out.
INT. WILKERSON PREP - HALLWAY - DAY
Claire walks among throngs of STUDENTS, greeting them with genuine affection. VICKY LANSING, 35, shapely and wearing tight clothes to prove it, comes up alongside her.
Ready for the little bastards?
A bit hostile for the first day.
Why wait? It's going to come to that soon enough.
Vicky pulls a chunk of drywall from Claire's hair.
Prowling construction sites in your spare time?
That garage door is acting up again.
Ethan's got a hot buddy who's incredibly handy with "domestic repairs." No thanks.
If you change your mind, I'm sure this guy could get it up for you.
Claire laughs and heads off.
I'll take that as a solid maybe.
INT. WILKERSON PREP - CLASSROOM - DAY
Claire enters as the bell rings. reluctantly take their seats. BOISTEROUS STUDENTS
For those of you who don't know me, I'm Mrs. Peterson. Welcome to Ancient Greek Literature. Moans from some of the kids.
I know. Torture, right? Trust me, you ain't seen nothin' yet.
On a large screen she projects a DRAWING of ancient Greeks forcing a man into a large HOLLOW BRASS BULL.

This, my friends, is the brazen bull. A little torture device conjured up by the ancient Greeks to roast criminals alive. And just to amp up the creepy factor, they put a network of pipes inside so the victim's screams would sound like the bellowing of an infuriated bull. Eeewww...
Disgusting ... Awesome.
They were pretty sick and twisted back then. Lucky for us, they wrote about it.
She sits on her desk and makes eye contact with each of them.
Jealousy. Ambition. Greed. Lust. Thousands of years ago, the Greeks were intimately familiar with the dark side of human nature. I'm betting that by the end of this class, you will be too.
She scans their riveted faces and knows she's got them.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - DAY
Next door, BURLY MEN unload furniture from a moving truck. Claire's SUV pulls in. see the activity. Claire and Kevin get out and crane to
I wonder who's moving in.
Maybe a cute girl your age.
Kevin rolls his eyes and goes inside. chuckle. Claire follows with a
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT

Claire loads the dishwasher: two plates, two glasses, two forks. She sorts through the mail, tossing aside envelopes addressed to GARY PETERSON. She comes to one addressed to her from "The Law Offices of Sharon Atkinson." She opens it and finds a PETITION FOR DIVORCE with a note attached: "Sign ASAP so I can file on your behalf."
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - MASTER BATHROOM - NIGHT

Claire, in a nightgown, brushes her teeth. herself in a full length mirror. She glimpses She pulls up her nightgown and stares with clinical detachment at her buttocks, turning to catch every angle. She drops her nightgown and spits into the sink.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - MASTER BEDROOM - NIGHT

Claire peers out at the newly occupied house next door. A light shines through the SEMI-SHEER CURTAINS in the window directly across from hers. Suddenly, behind the curtains a MALE SILHOUETTE appears, removing his shirt over his head. Claire draws her curtains. She gets into bed, careful not to disturb the other side, and turns out the light. She buries her face in the pillow next to hers and smells it. She closes her eyes.
INT. UPSCALE DEPARTMENT STORE - DAY
Claire and Vicky browse in the shoe department.
So file the damn thing already. I will.
I'm just not there yet.
What you need is somebody to get you there. A hot stud who's gonna barge in and shake you up and make you feel ... you know ... Desirable.
I was thinking more like horny.
It's meant as a joke but Claire remains gloomy.
You are desirable. He just had his head too far up his ass to appreciate it.

Vicky stops at a the sale rack, while Claire wanders over to a display of SEXY STILETTOS. Claire picks up one of the stilettos and stares at it, captivated by its raw sex appeal. A SALESMAN approaches.
Can I bring you your size? Oh no.
These aren't really my --
(to the Salesman) She takes a seven.
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV (MOVING) - DAY
Claire drives as Vicky touches up her makeup in the mirror.
I'll never wear them.
We'll see ...
Claire pulls into her driveway and sees a TEENAGE BOY playing basketball with Kevin. He's tall and well-developed -- more a man in appearance than a boy. This is NOAH SANDBORN, 17.
Who the hell is that?
I have no idea.
They watch as he dribbles circles around Kevin, his every muscle flexing. Vicky scrambles out of the car.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - DRIVEWAY - DAY
Vicky approaches as Noah shoots a perfect layup. Bravo!
Both boys turn to her, and for the first time we clearly see: NOAH'S FACE Magnetic eyes. Confident smile. Boyish, yet possessing a solemn quality that suggests an old soul living deep inside.
Vicky ignores Kevin and zeroes in on Noah. Hi there.
Vicky Lansing.
Noah Sandborn. Nice to meet you.
The pleasure is all mine.
Claire walks up.
Noah, this is my mom. (to Claire) Noah just moved in next door. Milwaukee.
Welcome to the neighborhood, Noah.
Noah's eyes come alive as they take Claire in.
Thanks a lot, Mrs. Peterson.
Oh please, call me Claire.
Noah's gonna be a senior at
No kidding. So you must be what? Eighteen already?
Not until June.
Okay, Vicky. Why don't we let the boys play?
Claire Vicky into the house. Noah watches them go with more than mild interest. Kevin notices.
Yeah, Vicky's something, isn't she? All the guys call her "The Second Coming." Get it?
That's pretty rude, man.
Oh no, I didn't mean it like -NOAH Chill, bro. Just messin' with you.
Noah steals the ball from Kevin and slam dunks it.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - LAUNDRY ROOM - DAY

Claire sorts laundry when she hears the sound of a car pull in. She looks out to the driveway. Her face darkens. GARY PETERSON, 45, gets out of a BLACK MERCEDES. Athletic and slightly graying, he's the picture of success in his Armani suit -- until you glimpse the melancholy look in his eyes.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - BACK DOOR - DAY
Claire swings the door open just before Gary knocks.
Kevin called. Said the garage door
I've already scheduled someone to come out. I'm here.
Let me take a look.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - GARAGE - DAY
Gary stands on ladder and pokes around inside the motor of the garage door opener. Claire watches from below, arms folded.
Motor's not burned out. Hand me a Phillips head, would you?
She does, her eyes travelling the lines of his buttocks.
I thought you were out of town.
Just a quick trip to meet with a new client. Let's see if that did it.
He climbs down and pushes the button.
There we go ...
What?
GARY
The door rises.
Your meeting. Where was it? New York.
Claire lets out a disgusted laugh.
I didn't see her.
We're separated, Gary. You're free to see whomever you want.
We're still married as far as I'm concerned. And I don't have any interest in seeing her or anybody else. I haven't been with anyone this whole time. I swear.
Wow, what a feat.
Don't be like that.
Like what, Gary? You want me to believe every word that comes out of your mouth? I tried that. Didn't exactly work out. Claire ...
Thanks for your help with the door.
Tell Kevin I'll call him about the camping trip.
Gary trudges out the garage door. Claire pushes the remote button and the door descends between them.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - DAY

Claire cuts ROSES from a hedge in her driveway when Noah comes out of his house with a MAN, 55, in a suit and tie. Claire watches from behind the hedge as they walk to an AUDI parked in Noah's driveway.
Promise you'll call me if there's any problem?
Don't worry.
You're going to love Wilkerson. Just wait. You'll have a slew of friends before you know it.

Their conversation becomes inaudible as the man gives Noah an affectionate slap on the back and gets into his car. Noah waves as the man drives off. He heads back to the house and spots Claire, who pretends to be minding her own business.
Hey, Claire. Those are some roses.
I'm impressed.
My mom studied horticulture in college. Rubbed off, I guess.
Maybe she can tell me how to get rid of these brown edges.
She died a few years ago in a car accident.
Oh ... I'm sorry. It's just you and your dad, then?
She nods to where the car had been parked.
Oh. Yeah. But he travels a lot for work, so I'm pretty much on my own.
You must get lonely.
I'm used to it. You get used to anything if you have to.
He suddenly looks bereft, weary. Claire smiles with empathy.
Well, see you around.
Noah heads back to his house. motherly concern in her eyes. Claire watches him go,
Hey, Noah? You're welcome to join us for dinner tonight.
Noah turns to her with a smile.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - KITCHEN - EVENING
Claire and Kevin watch with amusement as Noah devours his meal.
You didn't tell me your mom was such an incredible cook. I might have to crash dinner every night.
You're welcome anytime.
Claire spoons more potatoes onto Noah's plate, her BREASTS swaying with the motion. Noah's eyes take them in.
So I hear your Ancient Greek Literature class is pretty amazing.
Then you haven't heard her nickname. Kevin ...
Seriously, they call her the "The Crusher."
(to Claire) You cover Homer?
He's my favorite.
You're kidding, right?
Dude, you ever read the Iliad Achilles is this total bad ass. Hot-tempered, full of pride, totally consumed by his emotions.
Which proved to be his downfall.

Downfall? The guy was one of the greatest warriors of all time. People have been talking about him for centuries from Dante and Shakespeare to Zeppelin and Dylan. He's in comic books, video games, movies -- every modern day medium there is. Hell, I'll take that kind of failure any day. Wow, man.
You're really into it.
Claire's eyes gleam -- finally a kid who gets it.
(to Claire) You think I got a shot at getting into your class?
It's an honors class. Involves a ton of work. You think you can handle it?
Oh, absolutely.
Noah grins enthusiastically, ready for any challenge.
I'll see what I can do.
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV (MOVING) - MORNING
Claire drives while Kevin and Noah sit in back playing with their iPhones. Kevin notices NOAH'S SCREEN, which displays custom themes and applications.
(re: Noah's iPhone) Dude, what the heck?
Yeah, I jailbroke it. get all my cool apps.
That's how I
Kevin watches with fascination as Noah starts up a strange thirdparty application.
Is that legal?
It voids the warranty, but it's legal. I'll show you how after school if you want.
Here we are.
Claire pulls through the Wilkerson Prep gates. Noah looks out the window at the manicured grounds. smiles with deep satisfaction.
EXT. WILKERSON PREP - PARKING LOT - MORNING
As Kevin pulls his cello from the back of the SUV, Claire notices Noah's tie hanging loose around his neck.
You'll get detention if that's not tied properly.
I tried. I've just never gotten the hang of it. Here.
Claire straightens Noah's collar, her fingers brushing against his skin.
Chin up a little.
She wraps and loops the tie, her face close to his. at her eyelashes, her lips. He gazes
There you go. Perfect.
She smooths the tie and smiles at him.
Their eyes linger on each other for a long moment, something passing between them. Claire's smile slowly fades.
We're going to be late. Yes.
Better get going. Come on.
They head off.
I'll show you around.
Claire blinks, unsettled.
INT. WILKERSON PREP - HALLWAY - MORNING
Noah follows Kevin, who grapples with his cello case through a mob of students.
Need a hand? I got it.
Kevin consults a slip of paper and leads Noah to a locker.
Here it is.
Noah works the combination when Jason Zimmer comes around the corner, a couple of his STOOGES in tow. He spots Kevin.
Whatcha got in the case, Geek Boy?
Come on, Jason.
Maybe it's a AK47.
That right? You goin' Columbine on us?
Believe me Zimmer, you'd be the first to know.
Oh, Geek Boy's trying to be funny. You think you're funny? Huh?
Jason grabs the cello, but Kevin hangs on tight. They grapple with it amidst hoots and from the stooges. Suddenly, a HAND goes to Jason's neck. Let go.
NOAH
Jason writhes in Noah's vice grip, then finally releases the cello. Noah shoves him, nearly sending him to the floor.
What the -Jason wheels around ready for battle, but the cold-blooded look in Noah's eyes instantly unnerves him.
Fuck with him, you fuck with me.
Noah slams his locker and saunters off. shit-eating grin and follows. Kevin gives Jason a
INT. WILKERSON PREP - CAFETERIA - DAY
Claire and Vicky enter the food line.
Wouldn't you know I got friggin' Cameron Knopf in my class. He's just as big a tard as his older brother.
Claire's eyes drift to the dining room where she spots Allie Cambridge flirting mercilessly with Noah.
You'd think those parents would see what they got the first time around and be scared shitless to reproduce again.
Claire watches their interaction with a proprietary look. Vicky follows her gaze.
Figures she'd sniff him out on day one. Like a dog to raw meat.
Vicky chuckles, but Claire turns and walks the other way.
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV (MOVING) - DAY
Claire drives with Kevin beside her and Noah in back. Kevin spots Jason Zimmer lighting up a cigarette as he ambles down the street with his friends.
There he goes, douche bag. Kevin.
Well he is and you know it.
Did something happen?
It was no big deal.
(to Noah) You should have seen his face when you grabbed him, man. He looked like he was gonna pee his pants.
Better be careful, Noah. Jason's not someone you want as your enemy.
He's not as tough as he thinks.
Claire smiles to herself.
So how about friends? those today, Noah? You make any of
Not really.
I saw you talking to Allie Cambridge in the cafeteria.
She studies him in the mirror, but his face gives away nothing.
Man, I've been working up the nerve to talk to her for a year and you do it your first day.
She's a very pretty girl. Very.
She's just not my type.
Dude, she's every guy's type.
She's a little immature, that's all.
Noah's eyes meet Claire's in the mirror. She quickly looks away.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - MASTER BEDROOM - NIGHT

Claire, dressed in a nightgown, turns down the bed. She goes to draw the curtains and sees Noah reading in the window across from hers. Noah looks up, smiles and waves. Claire waves back, embarrassed, and closes her curtains.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - DRIVEWAY -N DAWN
Claire stretches, dressed for a run. Morning.
NOAH (O.S.)
She turns and sees Noah in his driveway in sweats and sneakers. Noah.
I see you out here every morning. Thought maybe you could show me the best trail.
She hesitates.
But I don't want to intrude on your private time or anything ...
Think you can keep up?
EXT. WOODS - MORNING
Claire and Noah run side by side, their feet pounding in sync along the path we've seen Claire travel. When they near the clearing, Claire points to the fallen sycamore.
There's the finish.
She sprints ahead and leaps over the tree, showing off. follows. Claire trots in place, gulping water. gulping air. Noah bends over,
Oh man ... you do this everyday?
Rain or shine.
Noah hangs on the tire swing and watches Claire stretch against the fallen sycamore.
It's nice here.
Peaceful.
Better stretch or you'll be sorry tomorrow.
Noah joins her at the tree, positioning himself near the carving of Claire's and Gary's initials.
(re: the carving) You do this?
Kevin's dad. Back when the tree was still standing.
Talk about symbolism.
Yeah, funny the way things work out.
Kevin said there's a chance you two might get back together.
I'm sure that's what every kid whose parents split up hopes for.
Well, you never know. My mom and dad almost got divorced but they worked it out in the end.
There are some things that can't be worked out.
Noah watches her as she struggles to hide her pain.
You're not really over him, are you?
What makes you say that?
Well, this place is obviously special to you -Was --
You run here every day ...
It's right behind the house. The trail's exactly six miles to this point.
I'm just saying ...
I'm the one who ended it.
People do all kinds of crazy things when it come to love. I don't.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - DRIVEWAY - DAY
Gary and Kevin load camping gear into Gary's car.
I think we've got everything. Wait.
I forgot my PSP.
You can't bring your PSP. Why not?
Because ... it's camping.
My point exactly.
He runs inside, passing Claire as she comes out with a duffle bag.
I packed his inhaler and some
prednisone in case his allergies
Why don't you come with us? It wouldn't have to mean anything.
Everything means something, Gary.
Noah appears, shirtless, lugging a ladder up his driveway.
Hey, Claire. Hi, Noah.
Noah nods to Gary, then goes into his garage.
Who's that?
Our new neighbor. He and Kevin have become fast friends.
Isn't he a little old for Kevin?
He's seventeen. A senior at
And you let him -Gary stops abruptly as Noah comes out of the garage.
waits for Noah to go into his house, then:
And you let him call you "Claire?"
Before she can answer, Kevin appears with his PSP, his iPhone, and his laptop.
You think they have internet?
You can bring the PSP, that's it.
Kevin begrudgingly hands the rest over to Claire.
Don't worry, it'll all be here when you get back.
She gives Kevin a kiss as he gets into the car.
You sure you're going to be okay here all by yourself?
I'm a big girl, Gary.
Gary gets in the car and pulls out. Claire waves goodbye.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - MASTER BATHROOM - DAY
Claire lies under the bathroom sink struggling to turn a pipe with a wrench. Water sprays out at her. Damn it.
The doorbell rings.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
Claire opens the door to find Noah palming a basketball.
Is Kevin around?
He went camping with his father.
Noah notices her wet hair, the annoyed look on her face.
Everything all right?
Yeah, fine.
Okay, I'll see you later.
He turns to go.
You know anything about plumbing?
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - MASTER BATHROOM - DAY
Noah lies on his back under the sink. Claire stands above him, her eyes drawn to the narrow slice of his BARE ABDOMEN revealed by his raised shirt.
This piece is corroded.
See? She bends down to look, her face hovering near his stomach.
I can stop the leak for now but
you'll need to replace it. He turns the wrench and lets out a LOW, GUTTURAL GROAN of exertion. Claire quickly stands, flustered. Noah comes out and gets to his feet, his face flushed.
Want me to get you the part?
No, that's okay.
I'll handle it.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - MASTER BEDROOM - DAY
Claire leads Noah past the bed and toward the door.
I talked to Mr. Warren about your transferring to my class. He says it shouldn't be a problem. Great.
Thanks.
Claire continues on, but Noah stops when he sees a DRESS laid out on the bed with the SEXY STILETTOS Claire purchased still in their open box.
You going out tonight?
With Vicky ...
He picks up one of the stilettos, examines it.
Wow, these are something.
(embarrassed) Vicky ... Ms. Lansing talked me into them.
I could see her wearing these ...
But not me.
I didn't mean -CLAIRE I'm probably going to return them.
She takes the shoe from him and shoves it back in the box.
What I meant was ... never mind. What?
Tell me.
It's just that some women don't need this kind of thing to be ... desirable.
Claire laughs, her face flushing with embarrassment.

I'm no expert or anything, but I've always thought true sex appeal is intangible. Something that comes from inside. Women like Vicky try to be sexy by wearing things like this. But it's really all just smoke and mirrors.
Plenty of men seem to go for that.
Plenty of men are idiots. Any guy who's worth your time isn't going to fall for these gimmicks. (re: the shoes) These are hot, no doubt. But you don't need them. You're the real deal.
Claire stares at him, at a complete loss for words.
I probably shouldn't CLAIRE
I was totally out of line.
It's okay. In fact, it's the nicest thing anybody's said to me in a very long time.
INT. UPSCALE SUSHI RESTAURANT - NIGHT
Claire and Vicky enter a crowded bar area. Claire wears the dress that was laid out on her bed, but instead of the stilettos, she wears a sensible pair of pumps.
I can't believe I let you talk me into this.
You're going to love him. He's cute, he's rich, and best of all
he's got all his hair. Vicky undoes the next button on Claire's dress. Relax.
You look fabulous.
Vicky waves vigorously at TWO ATTRACTIVE MEN across the room.
INT. UPSCALE SUSHI RESTAURANT - NIGHT

Vicky and her boy-toy, ETHAN, 29, feed each other sushi while practically sitting in each other's lap. Claire sits next to TRAVIS, 35, with her purse wedged firmly between them. Travis does indeed have all his hair, but he looks like the kind of guy who takes two hours to get it just right.
All our rooms are booked solid for the next eighteen months thanks to that write-up in Conde Nast.
It used to be a convent until
Travis got a hold of it.
Can you imagine? like that?
Prime real estate
Putting defenseless nuns out on the street. I don't know how you sleep at night, man.
Hey, they're the ones who took a vow of poverty, not me.
Vicky and Ethan laugh, but Claire musters only a tight smile.
(to Claire) Speaking of vows of poverty, I hear you're a teacher.
That's right.
What do you teach?
Classical Studies, specializing in Ancient Greek Literature.
Really? I didn't think they still taught stuff like that in high school.
What's that supposed to mean? Claire ...
Well, I mean let's face it. How many kids are really going to use what they learn in "Classical Studies?"
As he speaks, Travis's eyes flit to a CURVACEOUS WOMAN in a SKINTIGHT SKIRT who gets up from a nearby table. It's barely a glimpse, but Claire notices.
Kids nowadays need to be prepared for the realities of the job market. They need practical skills. Skills?
Yeah, a trade like plumbing or machine repair. For the brighter ones, technology, finance. That's where the money is. Ah, money.
That's the goal then.
Nothing wrong with that.
Except when it's motivated by nothing more than greed.
Ooooh, we've got ourselves a live one here. You're a little firecracker when you get riled up, aren't you?
You know, maybe we should order a little more -CLAIRE Seriously? A "firecracker"? Is that how you regard women who have an opinion on something.
Only if it disagrees with mine.
He laughs, trying to lighten the mood. Vicky joins in, a little too boisterously. Claire gathers her belongings.
It's getting late and I've got an early morning. It was a pleasure meeting you, Travis. See you tomorrow, Vicky.
Claire, please ...
Claire starts to leave. Suddenly, she turns back to Travis.
What?
Billionaire. Classics major.
She walks off in a huff.
EXT. SUSHI RESTAURANT - PARKING LOT - NIGHT
Light rain falls as Claire hurries to her car. after her. Vicky chases
Claire, wait!
Claire stops at her car, searching for her keys.
What the hell was that?
Come on, Vicky. You can't possibly see me with that guy.
So you pick a fight with him?
He deserved it.
What is your problem? Can't you just lighten up and have some fun?
Maybe I'm not ready for "fun."
Then sit at home alone and mope if that's what you want. But I guarantee you that's not what Gary's up to.
As a matter of fact, Gary told me he hasn't been with anyone.
Oh, please. He screwed around when you were together. You think he's suddenly become celibate now that you're apart?
Thanks a lot, Vicky.
Claire opens her car door, but Vicky stops her.
I'm sorry. But this limbo thing you're wallowing in -- it's just
not healthy. It's time to move on. Claire gets in, slams the door, and speeds away.
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV (MOVING) - NIGHT
Rain pelts the windshield as Claire drives. Toto's "I'll Be Over You" plays on the radio. Claire sings along, her voice cracking with emotion.

Remembering times gone by, Promises we once made, What are the reasons why, Nothing stays the same, As soon as my heart stops breaking, Anticipating, As soon as forever is through, I'll be over you ...
INT. CLAIRE'S SUV - NIGHT
Claire pulls into her driveway, her headlights slicing through the rain. She pushes the garage door remote. The door rises about a foot, then stops.
God damn it.
EXT. PETERSON HOUSE - DRIVEWAY - NIGHT

Claire gets out of the car and struggles to lift the door. She raises it several feet when it slips out of her hands and comes crashing down. She stares at it in disbelief, then lets loose on it, kicking it and beating it with her fists, until she breaks into sobs. Claire.
NOAH (O.S.)
Claire turns and sees Noah silhouetted by the headlights.
I can't ... It's broken. Noah gently pulls her clear, then thrusts the door up with a spectacular show of force.
INT. PETERSON HOUSE - GARAGE - NIGHT
